script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for SOXX. Monday — here is your SOXX recap. The portfolio closed up zero point eight seven percent on the session. A broad-based advance in semiconductor names carried the day, though not every holding participated.

Advanced Micro Devices unveiled the Helios AI rack server platform Monday, a direct challenge to Nvidia's infrastructure dominance. The question now is whether execution can match ambition — because the margin for error is razor thin.

The Nasdaq composite held its ground as the session's best-performing major index, outperforming the Dow, which retreated after President Trump's comments regarding Iran unsettled risk sentiment late in the afternoon. The S&P 500 is running up eleven point four percent year-to-date through July tenth, according to ETF Trends, keeping large-cap technology in position for a potential fourth consecutive year of double-digit gains.

The top story Monday belongs to Advanced Micro Devices and its Helios AI rack announcement. Yahoo Finance's technology editor Dan Howley covered the platform reveal, describing it as the clearest signal yet that Advanced Micro Devices is mounting a serious infrastructure-level challenge to Nvidia in the AI data center market. That framing is important: this is not a single chip launch, it is a full rack system play, competing on the terms Nvidia set. However, a Seeking Alpha analysis published Monday carries a sell rating on Advanced Micro Devices, arguing the bull case essentially requires Nvidia to stumble and that current valuations leave almost no margin for positive surprise. The Helios platform is a credible step forward. Whether it translates into meaningful share gains is the unresolved question, and it will likely stay unresolved until data center customers begin signaling procurement decisions.

Advanced Micro Devices was up one point five eight percent on the session. The Helios rack reveal dominated the tape for the name, and separately, a Yahoo Finance market recap noted that Advanced Micro Devices joined a broader AI stock comeback Monday as investors shook off recent trade-related anxiety. Watch for any customer or partner commentary on the Helios platform in the days ahead.

Micron Technology was up one point nine four percent on the session. Seeking Alpha published an upgrade to buy, citing the eighteen billion dollar float, leadership in SOCAMM two technology, over one hundred billion dollars in remaining performance obligations, and capital return activity as catalysts that could lift earnings per share through fiscal year twenty twenty-seven. Separately, a Yahoo Finance piece noted that Micron shares have been under pressure since early July, raising a dip-buying question. Raymond James strategist Matt Orton added context, projecting AI capital expenditure will surpass one trillion dollars by twenty twenty-seven, while warning chip stocks could face a fifteen to twenty percent pullback if major technology firms fail to raise spending guidance. That is the binary risk sitting over Micron right now.

Nvidia was up zero point two three percent on the session, a notably muted move relative to peers. News flow around the name Monday was largely indirect — Microsoft's underperformance and Amazon's valuation narrative drew more attention than Nvidia itself. Watch Big Tech earnings guidance closely, as Orton's warning about capex signals applies directly here.

Broadcom was up one point nine eight percent on the session. No company-specific news drove the move — the gain appears broad-sector in nature, consistent with data center infrastructure demand supporting the custom silicon franchise. Custom accelerator design win momentum remains the key variable to monitor.

Intel was up two point one three percent on the session, the strongest gain among the top five holdings. Yahoo Finance noted Intel and Advanced Micro Devices are both positioned to benefit from data center demand ahead of earnings season. Execution on the foundry business remains the central watch item.

Applied Materials was down zero point seven five percent on the session. No specific news drove the decline. The equipment sector faced some rotation pressure Monday, with the name modestly underperforming the broader semiconductor advance.

KLA Corporation was down two point four two percent on the session, the largest decliner across the primary holdings. No company-specific catalyst was identified. The move likely reflects equipment sector softness and possibly profit-taking following recent strength. Earnings visibility for the wafer inspection franchise remains intact longer term.

Marvell Technology was up three point three two percent on the session, the strongest performer in the portfolio on Monday. No single headline drove the move, but the name has been closely correlated with AI custom silicon demand trends, and Monday's risk-on tone in data center names lifted it broadly.

Lam Research was down two point zero nine percent on the session. Like KLA Corporation, the pullback appears sector-driven rather than company-specific. Deposition and etch equipment demand tied to advanced node investment remains the longer-term catalyst to track.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company was up zero point nine nine percent on the session. No direct news flow, but the broader AI capital expenditure outlook described by Raymond James supports continued advanced node loading at the world's leading foundry.

Three items to watch in the next forty-eight hours: First, any Big Tech earnings guidance revisions, which Raymond James flagged as a potential fifteen to twenty percent downside trigger for chip stocks. Second, customer commentary on the Advanced Micro Devices Helios platform from data center operators or cloud providers. Third, monitor Federal Reserve speaker appearances for any updated tone on rate trajectory, which directly affects semiconductor sector valuations.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for SOXX. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Keep close watch on AI capital expenditure signals — that is the variable with the most direct read-through to this entire portfolio.
